The hydrophobic effect that drives bilayer self-assembly is the same force students first meet stabilizing protein tertiary structure.
Amphipathic phospholipid orientation (hydrophobic tails in, polar heads out) mirrors the hydrophobic-core/polar-surface logic of folded proteins.
Carrier proteins are a concrete instance of the membrane transport proteins introduced as a protein function.
Channel proteins are membrane transport proteins; the protein-function category sets up the structural basis.
Facilitated diffusion via binding/release of solute depends on understanding reversible ligand binding.
Receptor-ligand recognition and location reasoning rests on binding-affinity/specificity from protein function.
Receptor tyrosine kinases are a specific receptor-protein type; the general receptor-protein concept is prerequisite.
Cell-surface receptors as membrane proteins build directly on the receptor-protein function category.
GPCR signaling cannot be understood without the G protein as a signaling protein.
Kinesin/dynein motors on microtubules are the same motor proteins introduced under protein function.
Actin-myosin interaction in the cytoskeleton relies on the myosin motor protein concept.
ATP-powered motor movement underlies actin-myosin force generation.
Actin filament structure builds on tubulin/actin as cytoskeletal structural proteins.
Microtubule tubulin structure builds on tubulin/actin as cytoskeletal structural proteins.
Actin polymerization dynamics presume knowing actin as a structural filament protein.
Collagen in the ECM is the same structural protein first taught under protein function.
Elastin/elastic fibers in connective tissue build on elastin as a structural protein.
Cell adhesion molecules as a protein function directly underlie the CAM section.
Cadherins are a specific CAM type; the CAM protein-function concept is prerequisite.
Integrins are CAMs; understanding them depends on the adhesion-protein concept.
Membrane glycoproteins/glycocalyx build on the glycoprotein conjugated-protein concept.
Primary active transport is defined by ATP hydrolysis driving uphill transport.
The Na+/K+ ATPase couples ATP hydrolysis to ion pumping; ATP-as-currency is prerequisite.
Secondary active transport couples a favorable ion gradient to uphill solute movement — the coupling concept generalized.
Passive diffusion down a gradient is the spontaneous (negative ΔG) case students must grasp first.
Calcium ATPase pumps require ATP-hydrolysis energy coupling.
Vesicle trafficking/exocytosis is an energy-requiring process grounded in ATP as energy currency.
Weak/optional: protein integrity concepts loosely inform tissue function — lower confidence.
S-phase IS DNA replication; the semiconservative replication mechanism is its content.
Cell-cycle telomerase is the same enzyme detailed under replication fidelity.
Telomere shortening / Hayflick limit follows directly from the end-replication problem.
Replicative immortality of cancer cells is the telomerase-cancer link applied.
p53 as a checkpoint regulator builds on the tumor-suppressor concept.
Cancer tumor-suppressor dysregulation directly reuses the tumor-suppressor gene concept.
Oncogenes/proto-oncogenes in cancer build on the same concept from gene regulation.
Differentiation by differential gene expression is the same-genome/different-expression idea applied to development.
Genomic equivalence rests on understanding that all cells share one genome but differ in expression.
Epigenetic control of cell fate builds on DNA methylation from gene regulation.
Tissue-specific transcription factors directing fate build on eukaryotic activators/TFs.
The prokaryotic operon concept is the direct foundation for the microbiology operon section.
The lac operon (inducible) is the same example developed under gene regulation.
The trp operon (repressible) reuses the repressible-system concept from gene regulation.
CAP/cAMP positive control in microbiology is identical to the gene-regulation concept.
Coupled transcription-translation in prokaryotes is taught first under transcription stages.
Polycistronic mRNA is introduced in transcription machinery and reused in prokaryote genetics.
Bacterial transformation as horizontal gene transfer builds on the recombination concept.
Phage-mediated transduction is the same mechanism in both sections.
Bacterial conjugation as HGT builds directly on the recombination conjugation concept.
Transposons in prokaryote genetics build on the jumping-genes concept from recombination.
Retroviruses in viral structure build on the retrovirus concept from molecular biology.
Understanding retroviruses requires the reverse transcriptase concept.
The lytic phage cycle builds on the lytic-vs-lysogenic distinction introduced in viral genetics.
The lysogenic phage cycle builds on the lytic-vs-lysogenic distinction.
Immunoglobulin-superfamily CAMs build on antibody/immunoglobulin protein structure.
Gap junction connexon assembly from subunits draws on quaternary subunit-assembly understanding.
Weak: covalent crosslinking concepts inform fibrous matrix proteins — lower confidence.
CDK kinase activity (phosphoryl transfer from ATP) presumes ATP as the phosphate/energy donor.
Ras oncogene activation by a point mutation builds on how sequence changes alter protein function.

Symbolic interactionism as a sociological theory rests on the same symbols-and-meaning premise introduced in self psychology.
Mead's account of the self is the foundation the symbolic-interactionist theory builds on at the macro level.
Goffman's dramaturgical approach is first taught in self-presentation, then reused as a pillar of symbolic interactionism.
Impression management is the same concept carried from individual self-presentation into the sociological frame.
Front/back stage distinction must be understood before applying it to sociological dramaturgy.
The social-construction-of-self mechanism prefigures the broader claim that reality is socially constructed.
Social exchange theory of helping is the micro version of the sociological exchange theory.
Cost-benefit/incentive reasoning underpins rational choice theory.
Understanding socialization is needed to grasp how institutions function as transmitters of norms.
Family as primary socializing agent is prerequisite to studying the family as a social institution.
School as socializing agent grounds the study of education as an institution.
Teacher-expectancy effect is a self-fulfilling prophecy applied in the classroom.
Media as a socializing agent is prerequisite to media as an institution.
Religion as socializing agent precedes its institutional functions.
The concept of a social norm is required before the cultural taxonomy of norms.
Folkways/mores/taboos are first defined in social influence and reused in the culture section.
Mores are introduced in the norms typology before the culture treatment.
Taboos are introduced in the norms typology before the culture treatment.
Sanctions as norm-reinforcers are first taught in socialization.
Values vs beliefs distinction precedes cultural values.
Beliefs as products of socialization ground cultural beliefs.
The Whorfian linguistic-relativity hypothesis is the same idea reframed as the Sapir-Whorf cultural concept.
Language structure grounds language as a carrier of nonmaterial culture.
Subculture is first introduced under collective behavior before the culture-variation section.
Counterculture is introduced under collective behavior before culture variation.
Ethnocentrism is the contrast needed to understand cultural relativism (within-section but ethnocentrism also seeded in PS.8B).
Ethnocentrism from prejudice section is prerequisite to its cultural-variation treatment.
Assimilation is first defined under collective behavior, then reused in cultural change.
Multiculturalism is defined earlier and reapplied to cultural change.

The situation component of social perception feeds the Thomas-theorem definition of the situation.
In-group/out-group from social identity grounds the sociological group typology.
Reference group from social identity is the same concept in the interaction section.

Conformity must be understood before group conformity dynamics.

Learned helplessness via perceived control links to self-efficacy (kept within-flow); excluded if not target.
